name: Credit Repair Express
description_short: The Tax Team Inc. offers credit repair and management services in Miami, focusing on disputing credit report errors, building better financial habits, and long-term credit health.
description_long: The Tax Team Inc. is a Miami-based financial services firm founded by CEO Aileen Guillen, claiming over 10,000 success stories and now serving clients nationwide. While primarily positioned as a tax preparation and accounting firm for entrepreneurs and professionals, the company prominently features credit repair and management as a core service offering alongside their tax services. Their credit repair approach centers on identifying inaccuracies in credit reports, disputing errors with creditors and bureaus, removing old late payments, and teaching clients sustainable financial habits. The company emphasizes a personalized, guided process that includes free credit assessments, dispute filing, fee and interest reduction strategies, and ongoing support to maintain improved scores long-term. What distinguishes The Tax Team is their integration of credit services with other financial tools like payroll services, budgeting services, and year-end accounting, positioning themselves as a one-stop financial solution rather than a standalone credit repair specialist. They highlight a 4.7 rating based on 200+ reviews across Google, Facebook, and BBB, and operate through a structured "playbook" process involving analysis, filing, communication, and continuous support. However, the website content is somewhat generic in places (repeated "best team of consultants" headers, incomplete FAQ section) and heavily emphasizes tax-related services, which may indicate credit repair is secondary to their primary accounting business. Their claims about timeline (30-90 days for improvements) and results are standard industry messaging rather than company-specific guarantees.
pros: Free credit assessment and consultation with no apparent upfront cost
Integrated service model allows bundling credit repair with tax, accounting, and payroll services in one relationship
Emphasis on long-term credit management and financial habit building, not just dispute filing
4.7 rating across 200+ reviews on Google, Facebook, and BBB demonstrates some track record
Local Miami expertise with claims of 10,000+ success stories, now expanding nationally
Transparent process laid out in 'playbook' format: analysis → filing → support → ongoing maintenance
Commitment to keeping clients informed throughout the dispute and recovery process
cons: Primarily a tax and accounting firm—credit repair appears to be a secondary service line, not their specialty
Website lacks specific pricing information, contract terms, or fee structure for credit repair services
No detailed case studies, specific dispute examples, or success metrics beyond generic review counts
FAQ section is incomplete (cuts off mid-question), suggesting the site may not be fully developed or maintained
Repeated generic phrases ('best team of consultants' × 16 times) and marketing-heavy language reduces credibility
best_for: Miami-area entrepreneurs and small business owners who need integrated tax, accounting, and credit services
Clients with tax debt who also need credit repair and want to bundle services with one provider
Individuals seeking long-term credit management education alongside dispute and error correction
Clients who prefer a personalized, guided process with ongoing support rather than DIY credit repair
diagnosis: The Tax Team is best for Miami-area business owners or tax clients who need credit repair as part of a broader financial restructuring and want to work with one provider for multiple services. The main caveat is that this is fundamentally a tax and accounting firm offering credit repair as an ancillary service, not a specialized credit repair company—so expertise and focus may be divided, and their credit repair processes are not differentiated from standard industry practices.
services: Credit report analysis to identify inaccuracies and errors
Dispute filing with credit bureaus and creditors
Late payment removal from credit history
Integration with tax preparation and planning services
